name: Crab Calzone
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour mixture and yeast from the hot roll mix. Stir in water and oil until moistened.
2. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. With greased hands, shape into a ball and knead for 3 minutes or until no longer sticky. Divide the dough into 10 equal parts and cover loosely with plastic wrap and a cloth towel.
3. In a medium bowl, combine the remaining ingredients and mix well. On a lightly floured surface, roll each ball of dough to an eight-inch circle.
4. Spoon 1/3 cup filling over half of the dough, within 1 inch of the edge. Brush the edge with water.
5. Fold the dough in half over the filling, press the edges to seal, and flute decoratively.
6. Place the calzones on a greased cookie sheet and brush with oil. Bake at 400 degrees F for 25-30 minutes, or until brown.
Variation: To turn the calzone from a main dish to an appetizer, wrap 1 tablespoon of the filling in fillo dough wrappers.
